169:         launcherPath,
170:         bridgePath,
171:         dbDir: path.join(directOpencodeDir, 'skills', 'system-spec-kit', 'mcp_server', 'database'),
172:         packageJsonPath: path.join(directOpencodeDir, 'skills', 'system-spec-kit', 'mcp_server', 'package.json'),
173:       };
174:     }
175: 
176:     const parent = path.dirname(current);
177:     if (parent === current) {
178:       throw new CliProtocolError('Unable to locate .opencode/bin launcher assets from CLI path');
179:     }
180:     current = parent;

<content>
1: ---
2: title: "sk-code Skill-Benchmark Artifacts"
3: description: "Benchmark inputs and reports for sk-code, scored by the deep-improvement Lane C harness in router and live modes."
4: trigger_phrases:
5:   - "sk-code benchmark"
6:   - "skill-benchmark artifacts"
7:   - "sk-code routing benchmark"
8: ---
9: 
10: # sk-code Skill-Benchmark Artifacts
11: 
12: > Reports and inputs for benchmarking how well `sk-code` is routed, discovered, and used in practice, kept beside the skill they measure.
13: 
14: ---
15: 
16: ## 1. OVERVIEW
17: 
18: The deep-improvement Lane C harness benchmarks `sk-code` against its own `manual_testing_playbook` scenarios across five dimensions (D1 routing, D2 discovery, D3 efficiency, D4 usefulness, D5 connectivity). This folder holds the run inputs and the dual reports each run writes.
19: 
20: Two trace modes score the same playbook corpus:
21: 
22: - **router** is deterministic and offline. It replays the machine-readable router in `sk-code/references/smart_routing.md`. This is the CI gate.
23: - **live** dispatches each scenario through `cli-opencode` to a real model and grades the model's stated routing plus observed activation. This is the operator default for a true routing verdict.
24: 
25: ### Key Statistics
26: 
27: | Metric | Value |
28: |---|---|
29: | Corpus | sk-code `manual_testing_playbook` (28 deterministic scenarios across 8 categories, including `DR-001`..`DR-004`) |
30: | Trace modes | router (deterministic CI gate), live (real dispatch) |
31: | Latest router verdict | FAIL, aggregate 44/100 |
32: | Latest live verdict | CONDITIONAL, aggregate 71 |
33: | D4 usefulness (approximate) | about 49 |
34: 
35: ### How This Compares
36: 
37: | Tool | Checks | Use When |
38: |---|---|---|
39: | This benchmark | Whether the skill is routed, discovered, and useful in practice | Measuring real routing quality |
40: | `validate.sh` | Document shape and required sections | Checking spec-folder structure |
41: | `manual_testing_playbook` | Described behavior per scenario | Authoring or hand-running scenarios |
42: 
43: ---
44: 
45: ## 2. QUICK START
46: 
47: Run from the repository root.
48: 
49: Router mode (deterministic, no network):
50: 
51: ```bash
52: node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/shared/loop-host.cjs \
53:   --mode=skill-benchmark --skill=sk-code \
54:   --outputs-dir=.opencode/skills/sk-code/benchmark/router-final \
55:   --trace-mode=router
56: ```
57: 
58: Live mode (dispatches through cli-opencode, needs a configured provider):
59: 
60: ```bash
61: SKILL_BENCH_OPENCODE_MODEL=openai/gpt-5.5-fast SKILL_BENCH_OPENCODE_VARIANT=high \
62: node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/shared/loop-host.cjs \
63:   --mode=skill-benchmark --skill=sk-code \
64:   --outputs-dir=.opencode/skills/sk-code/benchmark/live-final \
65:   --trace-mode=live --advisor-mode=python \
66:   --scenarios=SD-001,LS-001,CS-001,RD-002,MR-001
67: ```
68: 
69: Expected result: a `verdict=` line on stdout plus `skill-benchmark-report.json` and `skill-benchmark-report.md` in the outputs dir.
70: 
71: ---
72: 
73: ## 3. STRUCTURE
74: 
75: ```text
76: benchmark/
77: +-- router-final/      # Current deterministic run (router mode)
78: +-- live-final/        # Current live run (cli-opencode) + d4-ablation.json
79: +-- baseline/          # Frozen pre-optimization snapshot, do not regenerate
80: +-- after/ full/ live/ # Earlier development runs, superseded by the -final pair
81: `-- fixtures/sk-code/   # Legacy synthetic fixtures, superseded by the playbook corpus
82: ```
83: 
84: | Path | Purpose |
85: |---|---|
86: | `router-final/` | Latest router-mode report (real-gold scoring, the CI gate) |
87: | `live-final/` | Latest live-mode report plus `d4-ablation.json` (skill-on vs skill-off usefulness) |
88: | `baseline/` | First sk-code run, kept as a before-snapshot, not reproducible |
89: | `fixtures/sk-code/` | Two legacy fixtures, no longer the default corpus |
90: 
91: ---
92: 
93: ## 4. READING THE REPORTS
94: 
95: Each run writes a matched pair:
96: 
97: | File | Content |
98: |---|---|
99: | `skill-benchmark-report.json` | Machine report: verdict, D1 to D5, funnel, ranked bottlenecks, scenario rows |
100: | `skill-benchmark-report.md` | The same report rendered for reading, generated from the JSON to avoid drift |
101: | `d4-ablation.json` | Usefulness deltas per scenario (skill-on score, skill-off score), stamped approximate |
102: 
103: Start with the `.md` file for the verdict and the ranked bottlenecks. Open the `.json` file for per-scenario detail.
104: 
105: ---
106: 
107: ## 5. TROUBLESHOOTING
108: 
109: | What you see | Cause | Fix |
110: |---|---|---|
111: | Live dispatch returns null after about 4 minutes | `xhigh` reasoning variant is too slow per dispatch | Set `SKILL_BENCH_OPENCODE_VARIANT=high` |
112: | `provider/model not found` or 401 in live mode | The provider is not configured | Run `opencode providers list`, then log in to the provider you name in `SKILL_BENCH_OPENCODE_MODEL` |
113: | Browser scenarios skip with `SKIP-NO-BROWSER` | `bdg` (Chrome) is unavailable | Install `bdg`, or accept the honest skip for non-Chrome legs |
114: | Router mode reports orphan references | A routable doc is not reachable from the router | Add it to `RESOURCE_MAP` or the always-loaded default in `smart_routing.md` |
115: 
116: ---
117: 
118: ## 6. RELATED RESOURCES
119: 
120: ### Related Skills
121: 
122: | Skill | Relationship | Use When |
123: |---|---|---|
124: | [`deep-improvement`](../../deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/SKILL.md) | Owns the Lane C benchmark harness | Running or extending the benchmark |
125: | [`sk-code`](../SKILL.md) | The skill under measurement | Reading or tuning the router being scored |
126: 
127: ### Related Documents
128: 
129: | Document | Purpose |
130: |---|---|
131: | [`smart_routing.md`](../references/smart_routing.md) | The machine-readable router the benchmark replays |
132: | [`/deep:skill-benchmark`](../../../commands/deep/skill-benchmark.md) | The command that drives a benchmark run |

(End of file - total 132 lines)
</content>

# sk-code Skill-Benchmark Artifacts

> Reports and inputs for benchmarking how well `sk-code` is routed, discovered, and used in practice, kept beside the skill they measure.

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

The deep-improvement Lane C harness benchmarks `sk-code` against its own `manual_testing_playbook` scenarios across five dimensions (D1 routing, D2 discovery, D3 efficiency, D4 usefulness, D5 connectivity). This folder holds the run inputs and the dual reports each run writes.

Two trace modes score the same playbook corpus:

- **router** is deterministic and offline. It replays the machine-readable router in `sk-code/references/smart_routing.md`. This is the CI gate.
- **live** dispatches each scenario through `cli-opencode` to a real model and grades the model's stated routing plus observed activation. This is the operator default for a true routing verdict.

### Key Statistics

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Corpus | sk-code `manual_testing_playbook` (28 deterministic scenarios across 8 categories, including `DR-001`..`DR-004`) |
| Trace modes | router (deterministic CI gate), live (real dispatch) |
| Latest router verdict | FAIL, aggregate 44/100 |
| Latest live verdict | CONDITIONAL, aggregate 71 |
| D4 usefulness (approximate) | about 49 |

### How This Compares

| Tool | Checks | Use When |
|---|---|---|
| This benchmark | Whether the skill is routed, discovered, and useful in practice | Measuring real routing quality |
| `validate.sh` | Document shape and required sections | Checking spec-folder structure |
| `manual_testing_playbook` | Described behavior per scenario | Authoring or hand-running scenarios |

---

## 2. QUICK START

Run from the repository root.

Router mode (deterministic, no network):

```bash
node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/shared/loop-host.cjs \
  --mode=skill-benchmark --skill=sk-code \
  --outputs-dir=.opencode/skills/sk-code/benchmark/router-final \
  --trace-mode=router
```

Live mode (dispatches through cli-opencode, needs a configured provider):

```bash
SKILL_BENCH_OPENCODE_MODEL=openai/gpt-5.5-fast SKILL_BENCH_OPENCODE_VARIANT=high \
node .opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/scripts/shared/loop-host.cjs \
  --mode=skill-benchmark --skill=sk-code \
  --outputs-dir=.opencode/skills/sk-code/benchmark/live-final \
  --trace-mode=live --advisor-mode=python \
  --scenarios=SD-001,LS-001,CS-001,RD-002,MR-001
```

Expected result: a `verdict=` line on stdout plus `skill-benchmark-report.json` and `skill-benchmark-report.md` in the outputs dir.

---

## 3. STRUCTURE

```text
benchmark/
+-- router-final/      # Current deterministic run (router mode)
+-- live-final/        # Current live run (cli-opencode) + d4-ablation.json
+-- baseline/          # Frozen pre-optimization snapshot, do not regenerate
+-- after/ full/ live/ # Earlier development runs, superseded by the -final pair
`-- fixtures/sk-code/   # Legacy synthetic fixtures, superseded by the playbook corpus
```

| Path | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `router-final/` | Latest router-mode report (real-gold scoring, the CI gate) |
| `live-final/` | Latest live-mode report plus `d4-ablation.json` (skill-on vs skill-off usefulness) |
| `baseline/` | First sk-code run, kept as a before-snapshot, not reproducible |
| `fixtures/sk-code/` | Two legacy fixtures, no longer the default corpus |

---

## 4. READING THE REPORTS

Each run writes a matched pair:

| File | Content |
|---|---|
| `skill-benchmark-report.json` | Machine report: verdict, D1 to D5, funnel, ranked bottlenecks, scenario rows |
| `skill-benchmark-report.md` | The same report rendered for reading, generated from the JSON to avoid drift |
| `d4-ablation.json` | Usefulness deltas per scenario (skill-on score, skill-off score), stamped approximate |

Start with the `.md` file for the verdict and the ranked bottlenecks. Open the `.json` file for per-scenario detail.

---

## 5. TROUBLESHOOTING

| What you see | Cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| Live dispatch returns null after about 4 minutes | `xhigh` reasoning variant is too slow per dispatch | Set `SKILL_BENCH_OPENCODE_VARIANT=high` |
| `provider/model not found` or 401 in live mode | The provider is not configured | Run `opencode providers list`, then log in to the provider you name in `SKILL_BENCH_OPENCODE_MODEL` |
| Browser scenarios skip with `SKIP-NO-BROWSER` | `bdg` (Chrome) is unavailable | Install `bdg`, or accept the honest skip for non-Chrome legs |
| Router mode reports orphan references | A routable doc is not reachable from the router | Add it to `RESOURCE_MAP` or the always-loaded default in `smart_routing.md` |

---

## 6. RELATED RESOURCES

### Related Skills

| Skill | Relationship | Use When |
|---|---|---|
| [`deep-improvement`](../../deep-loop-workflows/deep-improvement/SKILL.md) | Owns the Lane C benchmark harness | Running or extending the benchmark |
| [`sk-code`](../SKILL.md) | The skill under measurement | Reading or tuning the router being scored |

### Related Documents

| Document | Purpose |
|---|---|
| [`smart_routing.md`](../references/smart_routing.md) | The machine-readable router the benchmark replays |
| [`/deep:skill-benchmark`](../../../commands/deep/skill-benchmark.md) | The command that drives a benchmark run |
